observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management should undertake the following:

Require the MEEDO Manager to prepare an aging of accounts receivable and evaluate collectability of overdue accounts for possible impairment.
observations.managementAction:
The MEEDO has started with the aging of accounts receivables especially those that were considered as overdue. However, due to the volume of consumers tagged with overdue accounts and with its manual recording of all of its transactions, the management has difficulty in finishing timely the aging of receivables.  Currently, the MEEDO is servicing an approximate 4,300 consumers with 9 out of 16 barangays being integrated.

The MEEDO has already accounted the 2% monthly penalty for overdue accounts and has reflected separately in the individual water bills.  The management has also issued notices of disconnection and has taken action accordingly on those consumers who have not paid their dues even after notice.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to:

The LCE should exhaust all possible remedies by the management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers. If it is still impractical to do so due to lack or absence of records, file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has retrieved only the Annual Audit Reports (AARs) for years before 2015 which upon examination do not contain details of Accounts Receivables and Payables.  Hence, its beginning balances are lumped in one Subsidiary Ledger which reflects unreconciled amounts.

These receivables are identified as dormant in the municipality’s financial statements as these are non-moving for at least 10 years.
observations.reason: The management has not yet applied for request for authority to write-off dormant accounts pending the preparation of required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant so that the latter may record them separately.



The Municipal Accountant should maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures. The offices have coordinated and plan to solve said record deficiency during physical inventory taking for the one-time PPE cleansing as this activity will identify and will take into record all assets that are existing however unrecorded or derecognized in the LGU books and transferred to Registry of Public Infrastructure.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Communicate/coordinate with depository banks to rectify errors in the bank statements through issuing a letter indicating the errors observed by the Municipal Accountant.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare promptly all necessary adjusting and correcting entries to record all valid reconciling items reflected in the BRS.
Require the personnel responsible for the preparation of BRS from the Accounting Department to inform the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee one month before the check becomes stale. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries for stale dated checks amounting to ₱ 273,128.56.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Accountant so that the latter may record them separately.
The Municipal Accountant should maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures. These ledgers shall be kept in the Accounting division and shall be maintained for each local road. Depreciation shall be computed separately and road lot component shall not be depreciated.
We further recommend that the accounting and reporting procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed, the required reports be submitted to this office and the road networks be properly disclosed in the Notes to the |Financial Statements of the Agency.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist as to the existence and whereabouts of the Breeding Stocks and if possible, secure documents related to the said accounts prepare adjusting entries when necessary. Moreover, prepare and maintain the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) as provided under Sections 114 of the NGAS Manual; and
The GSO Designate should include in the conduct of physical inventory the breeding stocks/animals in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate documentation of the breeding stocks inventory.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should refrain from recording as outright expense goods purchased which qualifies as inventory as defined and described under Paragraphs 9 and 11 of IPSAS 12. Recognize it as expense when the criteria set under Paragraph 44 of IPSAS 12 are met, such as when used, distributed, or when the related service has been rendered. Also, the Municipality shall implement the adoption of the Perpetual Inventory system.
The GSO should closely monitor the handling and distribution of goods held as inventory and prepare the required reports as to the receipt, distribution, and use of the supplies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the BAC to collate fuel requirements especially those used for project implementation and have it advertised for bidding. It was noted that most of the projects of the LGU were being implemented by administration. At the planning stage, the Municipal Engineering Office already have estimates of fuel requirements to be used by the LGU’s various heavy equipment. When it fails in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184, the LGU may proceed with the alternative modes of procurement provided by law in order to adhere with the principles of transparency, economy, and efficiency.
We also require that a justification be made on the direct purchase of gasoline from JCC Auto Fuel Station.

We recommended that the Management should:
Discontinue hiring of services which can be catered by employees of the agency.
Open and fund positions for the performance of daily operations instead of hiring consultants that perform regular functions.
Hiring of private lawyer shall be limited only to technical consultancy and no legal services must be rendered, otherwise the LGU shall avail of the services of the Provincial Legal Officer. However, in actions or proceedings where the municipality is a party adverse to the provincial government, a component city or another municipality, a private legal counsel or law firm may be hired but with prior written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and written concurrence of the Commission on Audit.
Review the provisions of R.A. 9184 and other related laws and regulations before hiring of consultants.

We recommended that the Management should:
Formulate and adopt internal rules and procedures on the rendition of overtime services which shall be circumscribed within the policies and guidelines of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2015.
Discontinue processing claims with incomplete documentation.
Stop charging payments of overtime services rendered by job order workers to Personnel Services. And since job order workers are paid on a no work no pay basis, might as well include services rendered on weekends in their daily wage computation, which shall be charged against MOOE.
Prepare a “Report on Overtime Services with Pay” using the template prescribed in the joint circular when there is payment of cash and submit the same to the DBM’s Budget and Management Bureau or Regional Office concerned, copy furnished the CSC, on or before March 31 of every year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the BAC to include in the instructions to suppliers that brand names should be indicated in the Request for Quotation (RFQ) and that the brand name of the winning bid be indicated in the purchase order to ascertain that the goods that will be delivered is the same as that indicated in the PO and to serve as reference during deliveries, inspection and acceptance by management. Moreover, validation of the reasonableness of the price offered will be easier.

•	With the implementation of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) effective January 1, 2015, the agency must conduct an inventory on all Property, Plant and Equipment; and corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers shall be prepared for the PPEs by the Accounting Office.

However, there is no existing list of all PPEs with amounts that corresponds to the amount reflected in the trial balance which made it difficult for the agency to identify which items are still in the trial balance that no longer exists physically.  Hence, the agency shall conduct an inventory of all PPEs which will reflect what is existent physically and write off those that no longer exist.
